resume:
机械工程本科在读 | Manufacturing Process Intern Candidate

Keywords: Lean Manufacturing, Six Sigma, Kaizen, 5S, Kanban, SPC, DMAIC, TPM, OEE, Poka-Yoke, Value Stream Mapping, Takt Time, CNC, CAD/CAM, SolidWorks, AutoCAD, GD&T, FMEA, PPAP, APQP, ISO 9001, IATF 16949, MES, ERP, SAP PP, Andon, Heijunka, Jidoka, root-cause analysis, fishbone, Pareto, cycle-time reduction, throughput optimization

核心技能 Core Skills:
- 熟悉 Lean / Six Sigma 方法论，课程项目中应用过 DMAIC 与 value stream mapping
- SolidWorks、AutoCAD 制图，了解 GD&T 公差标注
- Python / MATLAB 数据分析，可做 SPC 控制图
- 英语 CET-6，可读英文技术文档

项目经历 Projects:
- 校办工厂 capstone：与 4 人小组优化某夹具装配线布局 (line layout)，课程报告里写了 takt time 与 OEE 概念
- 课程作业：用 fishbone + Pareto 分析某虚拟产线 defect rate

实习 Internship:
- 某汽车零部件厂 暑期见习 6 周 (shadowing，无正式 KPI)

证书: Six Sigma Green Belt (online MOOC), SolidWorks CSWA
jd: We are hiring a Manufacturing Engineering Intern for a Tier-1 automotive components plant (stamping + assembly). You will support the process engineering team on the brake-caliper assembly line. Responsibilities: assist with time studies and takt-time balancing, help maintain SPC charts on critical-to-quality dimensions, participate in Kaizen events, and run small data-collection tasks under an assigned mentor. You will NOT own a line or a KPI; expectations are learning-oriented. Required: pursuing a degree in Mechanical/Industrial Engineering, basic familiarity with lean concepts, comfort on a shop floor (PPE, safety rules), and willingness to work first shift. Nice to have: SolidWorks, Python for basic data work. This is a 12-week paid internship; strong interns are considered for new-grad rotational offers.

history.q: Thanks for coming in. To start — why manufacturing, and why this brake-caliper line specifically?
Your resume lists a lot of lean tools. Which one have you actually used hands-on, not just read about?
Tell me about the capstone line-layout project. What was your specific piece of it?
You wrote 『takt time』 in the report — in your own words, what is takt time and why does it matter?
On that fixture line, what was the bottleneck station, and how did your group identify it?
How did you measure cycle time? Stopwatch, video, MES data?
During your 6-week shadowing at the auto-parts plant, what was one thing on the floor that surprised you?
Did you ever see an Andon pull happen? Walk me through what the operators did.
Let's do a small scenario. SPC chart shows three consecutive points trending up toward the upper control limit, none over yet. What do you do?
You mentioned a fishbone for defect analysis. Give me two of the categories you put on it.
If I asked you to do a time study on station 4 tomorrow morning, how would you set it up?
What PPE would you expect to wear on a stamping floor, and why?
Suppose your mentor is busy and an operator asks you whether a borderline part should ship. What do you say?
Tell me about a time you had to learn an unfamiliar tool or method quickly for a class project.
What does OEE stand for, and which of its three components do you think operators most directly affect?
Concretely: on the capstone fixture line, what change did your group recommend, and what was the before/after number?
Right — so what was the actual cycle-time or throughput delta after your recommended layout change? Give me the number.

candidate_last_answer: 嗯…其实那个 capstone 项目让我学到最多的是 teamwork。我们组里有个同学特别 push，开会经常吵起来，后来我们用了一个 group charter,大家分工才顺畅。我觉得 soft skills 在制造业其实被低估了 honestly。而且我那个学期还在准备 Six Sigma 的 online 考试,时间管理压力很大,我专门做了一个 Notion 的 schedule。所以我对自己的 time management 还挺有信心的,我相信来实习也能很快上手。

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: 所以我对自己的 time management 还挺有信心的

expected-yield: drag the candidate off the time-management tangent back to the asked question and force a concrete before/after cycle-time or throughput number from the capstone layout change, or an explicit admission that the project produced no measured result
